params.userRandomNumber: A random bytes32 you generate off-chain for added entropy.        Use: ethers.randomBytes(32) or any random hex bytes32.

CALLBACK_GAS_LIMIT().notice: Gas limit for Pyth callback — covers _findWinner loop for large snapshots

ENTROPY_CONTRACT().notice: Pyth Entropy contract on Monad Mainnet

ENTROPY_PROVIDER().notice: Pyth default provider on Monad Mainnet

_entropyCallback(uint64,address,bytes32).notice: Called automatically by Pyth oracle. Stores the random number onchain.         Intentionally cheap — winner selection happens in fulfillDraw().

fulfillDraw().notice: Call this after the Pyth callback fires (state = RandomnessReceived).         Runs _findWinner and sets the winner. Anyone can call.

getDrawFee().notice: Returns recommended MON fee for requestDraw(). Send this as msg.value.         Based on empirical testing — 0.6 MON worked on Monad mainnet.

getWalletTickets(address).notice: Returns a wallet's ticket range [from, to] inclusive.

requestDraw(bytes32).notice: Request randomness from Pyth Entropy. Send getDrawFee() as msg.value.
